user.title: Connect your Fire TV
user.description:
**Before you start:**
1. On your Fire TV, go to **Settings → My Fire TV → Developer Options** and enable **ADB Debugging**
2. Find your Fire TV IP at **Settings → My Fire TV → About → Network → IP Address**
3. Enter the IP below and click Submit
4. **Look at your TV screen** — approve the USB debugging prompt within 30 seconds

Make sure Home Assistant and Fire TV are on the same network.

data_description.host: Found at Settings → My Fire TV → About → Network
data_description.port: Only change if you modified the default ADB port
data_description.name: A friendly name for this device on your dashboard

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect. Verify the IP is correct, ADB Debugging is on, and you approved the prompt on your TV screen. Try again — you have 30 seconds to approve.

abort.already_configured: This Fire TV is already configured.

init.title: Fire TV Enhanced Settings
init.description:
Adjust polling intervals and customize app names.

To rename apps, add one per line in the format:
`com.example.app = My Custom Name`

Example:
`com.apple.atv = Apple TV`
`com.globo.globotv = Globoplay`

data.custom_apps_text: Custom app names (package = Name, one per line)

data_description.scan_interval: How often to check which app is running (2-60s, default 5)
data_description.screenshot_interval: How often to take a screenshot (5-120s, default 10)
data_description.custom_apps_text: Override app names shown on your dashboard. Check the 'App Package' sensor for the package name.
